reviews.review_msg: Honestly such a great prof. Very passionate about everything he talks about and gave us insightful knowledge about the material. If anything, he made us read 1 novel every 2 weeks, so there is ALOT of reading to get through by the end of the semester. I however was able to survive by skimming and using spark notes. Exams fair, just know the books.

Wouldn't recommend skipping lectures - no notes uploaded, lecture recording, or really anything supplementary sent out. Lectures can seem dull at times, but you gain a better understanding of the books and it will REALLY help your marks. Lots of reading so skimming the books might be more practical.
Chooses interesting course material. Lectures are straight forward but sometimes go off into tangents. Exam is super easy if you attend all the lectures and do all your readings.

Definitely a great first year prof. The content of the class was enjoyable and he was able to make boring lectures bearable. Adds a lot of humour and lame jokes into classes, which is appreciated. Overall, he was a really good prof and I would recommend anyone to take a class with him.

One of the sweetest, funniest, most interesting profs I've had. His lectures do pretty much consist of him talking about the works, but his thoughts really helped me understand the readings on a deeper level. I had him for the tutorial too and he was nothing but supportive of everyone's ideas. Offers useful feedback too, which I greatly appreciate.
Very interesting and definitely knows his stuff well... Highly recommended :)

Very good professor. He is very passionate and makes lectures very interesting, I'm usually scrambling to write down as much as I can. Is very easy to approach and to bounce ideas off of about poems and passages of novels. Very helpful when you don't understand something. Would definitely take another course with him.
His apocalyptic lit class is one of the best courses I've ever taken at SFU. Love his classic lecture style, would love to do a course like this again.

I was disappointed with this prof after reading such great reviews here. He spent a lot of time reading passages from novels, rather than explaining their significance etc. I felt as though more time should have been spent talking about the novels, rather than reading them to the class. Novels were interesting, nonetheless.
